title: Cylinder Object
description: Transient cylinder. A transient cylinder is not displayed or saved in a document. A transient cylinder is but is used as a wrapper to work with raw cylinder information. A transient cylinder has no boundaries and is infinite in length. They are created statically using the create method of the Cylinder class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Cylinder object.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets the data that defines the cylinder.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the data that defines the cylinder.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cylinder that defines its normal direction.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: origin
properties.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cylinder.

axis.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cylinder that defines its normal direction.

propertyValue = cylinder_var.axis

propertyValue = cylinder_var.evaluator

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = cylinder_var.objectType

origin.title: Cylinder.origin Property
origin.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cylinder.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cylinder_var.origin

# Set the value of the property.

classType.title: Cylinder.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Cylinder.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Cylinder object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Cylinder object that is a copy of this Cylinder object.

create.title: Cylinder.create Method
create.return_description: Returns the new Cylinder object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cylinder.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cylinder that defines its normal direction.

getData.title: Cylinder.getData Method
getData.description: Gets the data that defines the cylinder.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The output origin point (center) of the base of the cylinder.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The output center axis (along the length) of the cylinder that defines its normal direction.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the cylinder.

set.title: Cylinder.set Method
set.description: Sets the data that defines the cylinder.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The origin point (center) of the base of the cylinder.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: The center axis (along the length) of the cylinder that defines its normal direction.

transformBy.title: Cylinder.transformBy Method
transformBy.description: Updates this surface by transforming it with a given input matrix.
transformBy.return_description: Returns true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the surface.
